{{Quote|There is a new theme for Yoda, the teacher of the Jedi Knights. His theme begins in a kind of piquant way and then develops into a more profound, more noble piece.|John Williams|<ref name="Once Upon">''[[Once Upon a Galaxy: A Journal of the Making of The Empire Strikes Back]]''</ref>}}
'''Yoda's Theme''' is a recurring musical theme in the ''[[Star Wars]]'' saga, composed by [[John Williams]] and associated with [[Jedi Master]] [[Yoda]].

It was first used in the scenes on [[Dagobah]] in [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''The Empire Strikes Back'']], as well as during the hallway shootout scenes on [[Cloud City]]. In [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Return of the Jedi'']] it is used when [[Luke Skywalker]] revisits a dying Yoda.

The theme returns briefly near the end of the [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''The Phantom Menace'']], in which Yoda discusses the future of [[Anakin Skywalker]] with [[Obi-Wan Kenobi]]. In [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Attack of the Clones'']], the leitmotif is heard more often: first during the scene where Yoda is training the [[Bear Clan]], the conveyor belt sequence in the [[Geonosis|Geonosian]] droid factory, and finally when Yoda saves Obi-Wan and Anakin from a pillar toppled by [[Count]] [[Dooku]]. In [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Revenge of the Sith'']], it is played when Yoda leaves [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]], when he escapes with [[Bail Prestor Organa|Bail Organa]] after his duel with [[Darth Sidious]], and in a deleted scene in which Yoda arrives on Dagobah.

The theme is reused in [[Star Wars: Episode VIII The Last Jedi|''The Last Jedi'']], when Yoda's [[Force ghost/Legends|spirit]] appears to Luke and encourages him to learn from his failure with [[Ben Solo|Kylo Ren]].

The theme is played in [[Star Wars: Episode IX The Rise of Skywalker|''The Rise of Skywalker'']] when [[Luke Skywalker]] lifted his [[Red Five (T-65B X-wing starfighter)|''Red Five'']] X-wing and during the celebration of victory against the First Order, in the "[[Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker (soundtrack)|Reunion track]]."

A more dramatic, fast-paced variant of the main motif is heard during Yoda's duel with [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] in ''Attack of the Clones''. This version is used in ''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]'' as Yoda's theme when he is played as a "Hero" unit. Pieces of the motif in the track "Carbon Freeze/Darth Vader's Trap" play in ''[[LEGO Star Wars II: The Original Trilogy]]'' on the [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]] level.

The theme appears several times in the TV series ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]. '' It first appears in'' [[Ambush]]'', which featured Yoda as the main character; and in [[Assassin/Legends|''Assassin'']], when [[Ahsoka Tano]] tells Yoda about her visions of [[Padmé Amidala|Padmé]].

Yoda's leitmotif transitions to "[[Ahsoka's Theme]]," with which "Yoda's Theme" shares its opening notes, in the [[Star Wars Forces of Destiny|''Forces of Destiny'']] episode "[[Teach You, I Will]]," as Yoda teaches Ahsoka Tano to stay true to herself.

A snippet of the theme was used by [[Ludwig Göransson]] in the score for ''[[Star Wars: The Mandalorian]]''. A brief section of it is played in the [[2020]] episode "[[Chapter 13: The Jedi]]," when Ahsoka Tano describes once having known a being of [[Din Grogu|Grogu's]] [[Yoda's species|kind]]. The theme appears again in ''[[Star Wars: The Book of Boba Fett]]'', also scored by Göransson, when Luke Skywalker speaks about Yoda.
